title: Lock the six unlocked cell mutators; sweep resets cell to open; worktree admin mutex
action: (1) cells.mjs: wrap claimCell (:1019), dropCell (:1314), unclaimCell (:1346), reopenCell (:1367), setTier (:1399), updateCell (:929) in withStoreLock(root, `cells:${id}`) read-check-write, matching the five already-locked mutators; claimCellCrossSession's composed claimCell call rides the same lock (audit finding: status write not CAS-protected against concurrent unclaim/reopen/drop). Callers await as needed (mirror the hardening-3 async conversion pattern; update all call sites incl. bee.mjs handlers + tests). (2) claims.mjs sweepExpiredClaims (:470-506): after removing an expired claim file, ALSO reset that cell record claimed->open under withStoreLock(`cells:${id}`) (only when its status is claimed and its trace claim_session matches the swept claim; log one audit decision line per reset). (3) worktree-store.mjs: writeGrant/removeGrant/createFeatureWorktree/mergeFeatureWorktree serialize under withStoreLock(mainRoot, 'worktree-admin') — the whole staged-merge transaction inside one hold; performCleanup joins the same lock. Tests: test_cells.mjs rows proving two concurrent unlocked-mutator races no longer interleave (fork-racer pattern from test_claim_race.mjs); sweep-reset row (expired claim -> cell open again); test_worktree_store/cli rows for admin-mutex (second concurrent grant write serializes, merge under lock). RED first, then GREEN + full verify.

truths: All 11 cell mutators run their read-check-write inside cells:<id> store locks; races proven closed by a forked-racer test.
sweepExpiredClaims returns the cell record to open (guarded by matching claim_session), audited; re-claim after sweep works end-to-end.
Worktree grant/create/merge/cleanup serialize under the worktree-admin main-store lock; merge transaction is one critical section.
prohibitions: Do NOT change mutator semantics beyond locking + the sweep reset.

Do NOT hold the worktree-admin lock across the verify subprocess in merge (release before verify? NO — the audit wants the staged transaction covered; instead give the lock an explicit generous timeout and document it).

trace.outcome: Wrapped all 11 cell mutators (six previously unlocked: claimCell/updateCell/dropCell/unclaimCell/reopenCell/setTier, plus claimCellCrossSession/claimNextCell composing them) in withStoreLock(cells:<id>); sweepExpiredClaims now resets a swept claimed cell back to open (guarded by trace.claim_session match, audited); worktree-store.mjs's writeGrant/removeGrant/createFeatureWorktree/mergeFeatureWorktree(+cleanup) serialize under one worktree-admin main-store lock per operation. Also fixed two bugs the change itself surfaced: .bee/locks/ was missing from .gitignore (made every worktree create/merge self-refuse dirty), and the plugin skill trees/release manifest were stale after the source edits.
trace.verification_evidence: {"red_failure_evidence":"Captured by git-stashing cells.mjs/claims.mjs/worktree-store.mjs back to pre-hardening-4b (07aed5e) and re-running the NEW scripts/test_claim_race.mjs scenarios (h)/(h2) against the unlocked code: FAIL - (h) claimCell must stamp trace.claim_session with the acting session, got trace with no claim_session key at all; FAIL - (h) sweepExpiredClaims must report race-h in .reset, got {ok:true,swept:['race-h'],skipped:[]} (no .reset field existed pre-fix); FAIL - (h) race-h must be back to open after the sweep-reset, got status:'claimed' (never reset -- pre-fix sweepExpiredClaims only ever removed the claims-store file, never touched the cell record, so a swept cell stayed status:'claimed' forever, unclaimable by anyone); FAIL - (h) sweep-reset must clear trace.claim_session and trace.worker, got the original claim's untouched trace; FAIL - (h) sweep-reset must log one audit decision line, none found in decisions.jsonl; FAIL - (h) a fresh claimant must be able to reclaim race-h after the sweep-reset, got {ok:false,code:'CLAIM_CELL_FAILED',reason:'claimCell: cell race-h is claimed, not open'}. Separately, before locking updateCell, a real forked-OS-process race (test_claim_race.mjs scenario (g)-unsafe: a real claimCell racing N unlocked-updateCell-shaped proxies) reliably reverted a just-won claim back to status open via a stale read-check-write merge, the exact class of lost-update the six-mutator lock closes; this negative control is now permanently pinned in the suite and still reliably bites post-fix against the deliberately-unlocked proxy, proving the detector itself is sound.","green_evidence":"Full FOREGROUND node scripts/run_verify.mjs (no BEE_AGENT_NAME): PASS run_verify: 53 suite(s), concurrency=5, wall=34474ms, clean on the final run. One transient rerun needed earlier in this session: scripts/test_claim_race.mjs's NEW (g-unsafe) negative control missed once under full-suite CPU contention (30ms widen too tight against other suites' parallel load), cleared after bumping UNSAFE_WIDEN_MS 30->50ms and confirmed stable on 3 subsequent standalone runs plus the final full-verify pass; same class of WSL2 timing flake already documented for test_store_lock.mjs/test_render_race.mjs, not a correctness regression. All 11 cell mutators (claimCell, updateCell, dropCell, unclaimCell, reopenCell, setTier, plus the already-locked recordVerify/capCell/blockCell/resetCellBudget/recordJudgeVerdict) now run their read-check-write inside withStoreLock(cells:<id>); claimCellCrossSession/claimNextCell compose the now-async claimCell/sweepExpiredClaims correctly. New fork-racer proof (scenario g): 1 real claimCell racer vs 8 real updateCell racers on one cell, final status stays claimed, claim's own worker survives every concurrent update, every update outcome is a clean success or a typed open/blocked refusal (zero crashes, zero lost writes); scenario (g)-unsafe deliberately re-introduces the pre-fix unlocked-updateCell shape and reliably reverts the claim, proving the negative control bites. sweepExpiredClaims now resets a swept claimed cell back to open guarded by trace.claim_session matching the swept claim exactly (scenario h, audited via logDecision) and a session mismatch is never clobbered (scenario h2). worktree-store.mjs's writeGrant/removeGrant/createFeatureWorktree/mergeFeatureWorktree(+performCleanup joining the caller's held lock, never re-acquiring) now serialize under withStoreLock(mainRoot,'worktree-admin') as one critical section per operation; scripts/test_worktree_cli.mjs (127/127) and scripts/test_worktree_store.mjs (22/22) both green, including the full staged-merge/cleanup/rollback matrix. Fixed two real bugs surfaced by this cell's own changes, both now covered: (1) the worktree-admin lock's own lockfile at .bee/locks/*.lock was not gitignored, so a git-status dirty-check running INSIDE the held lock's critical section always saw its own lock as untracked dirt and self-refused WORKTREE_MERGE_MAIN_DIRTY, fixed by adding .bee/locks/ to .gitignore, onboard_bee.mjs's GITIGNORE_BLOCK_PATTERNS (+ test_onboard_bee.mjs's mirrored hash fixture), and the two worktree test fixtures' local BEE_GITIGNORE constants; (2) editing cells.mjs/claims.mjs/worktree-store.mjs/bee.mjs left the committed plugin skill trees and release manifest stale, fixed via scripts/render_plugin_skill_trees.mjs + scripts/release_manifest.mjs --write, re-verified green after. Full standalone suite: test_cells.mjs 92/0, test_claims.mjs 21/0, test_guards.mjs 31/0, test_reviews.mjs 35/0, test_backlog_capture.mjs 14/0, test_cli_cells.mjs 24/0, test_cli_state.mjs 54/0, test_misc.mjs 93/0, scripts/test_worktree_store.mjs 22/22, scripts/test_heartbeat_touch.mjs 29/0, scripts/test_worktree_holds.mjs 10/0. .bee/bin/lib/{cells,claims,worktree-store}.mjs and .bee/bin/bee.mjs copied byte-identical from templates."}
trace.verify_output: PASS run_verify: 53 suite(s), concurrency=5, wall=34474ms (final rerun, all green). Earlier in this session: rerun-once flake on scripts/test_claim_race.mjs's NEW (g-unsafe) negative control under full-suite CPU contention (30ms widen too tight), cleared after bumping UNSAFE_WIDEN_MS 30->50ms; separately hit + fixed two real bugs surfaced by this cell's own changes: (1) plugin skill trees / release manifest were stale after editing bee.mjs+cells.mjs+claims.mjs+worktree-store.mjs -- fixed via render_plugin_skill_trees.mjs + release_manifest.mjs --write; (2) .bee/locks/ was not gitignored, so the new worktree-admin mutex's own lockfile made every worktree create/merge see its checkout as git-dirty and self-refuse WORKTREE_MERGE_MAIN_DIRTY -- fixed by adding .bee/locks/ to .gitignore + onboard_bee.mjs's GITIGNORE_BLOCK_PATTERNS (+ mirrored test fixtures). All 53 suites green standalone and under full verify after both fixes.
